subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's domain is electricity — how it flows through circuits, what components make up circuits, and the effects of electric current (heating and magnetic). C-2.2 is an exact match: it covers describing how electricity works through manipulating different elements in simple circuits and demonstrating the heating and magnetic effects of electricity. The chapter covers circuit construction, conductors and insulators, switches, and the effects of current — all squarely within C-2.2's definition.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to describe how electricity flows through simple circuits by manipulating circuit components and identifying the heating and magnetic effects of electric current.
